tutorial.step_welcome_content: This quick tour covers the key features so you can start grading smarter. You can skip at any time and restart from Settings whenever you like.
tutorial.step_rubrics_content: Create rubrics with custom criteria, performance levels, and point scales. Link criteria to CEFR language standards, add a vocabulary list for automatic document analysis, or insert pre-built speaking dimensions. Version history lets you restore any previous rubric state. You can also click 'Compare' next to a rubric to grade two students side by side.
tutorial.step_students_content: Add students and organise them into classes. Each student gets a profile showing their grades, progress over time, and CEFR achievement level. You can also configure VO tracks per class.
tutorial.step_grading_content: Open a rubric from the Rubrics page and pick a student to grade. Score each criterion, attach evidence files, record voice feedback, or use the Comment Bank for reusable snippets. Hit 'Save & Next' to move through your class efficiently.
tutorial.step_comparative_content: On the Rubrics page, click 'Compare' to grade two students side by side. The app adaptively suggests adjustments based on their relative performance — ideal for calibration sessions and anchor-based grading.
tutorial.step_cefr_content: Track language proficiency across your entire school using the Common European Framework of Reference. See which CEFR levels each student has reached per skill, spot class-wide trends, and monitor progress over time.
tutorial.step_attachments_content: Upload PDFs, Word documents, or images as reference materials or student submissions. During grading, click 'Analyse' to automatically extract text via OCR and detect vocabulary words and grammar structures — powered by Tesseract and LanguageTool.
tutorial.step_statistics_content: View grade distributions, per-criterion performance, CEFR achievement rates, and learning goal progress across your class. Use these insights to spot trends and identify students who need extra support.
tutorial.step_comments_content: Build a reusable library of feedback snippets organised by tag. During grading, insert them with one click to save time and keep your feedback consistent.
tutorial.step_export_content: Export graded rubrics as formatted Word documents (using custom templates), PDFs, or CSV files. Export a single student or your entire class in one go.
tutorial.step_settings_content: Change the theme and accent colour, manage grade scales, set your language (English or Dutch), and configure the Standards API key. You can restart this tour at any time from the Settings page.
tutorial.grading_step_criteria_content: Each card is one grading criterion. Work through them top to bottom to score the student on every dimension.
tutorial.grading_step_level_content: Click a performance level to award that score. Use number keys 1–5 while a criterion is focused for fast keyboard grading.
tutorial.grading_step_comment_content: Click the speech-bubble icon to open the comment editor for this criterion. You can also pull snippets from your Comment Bank with one click.
tutorial.grading_step_modifier_content: Apply a global percentage or point adjustment to the final score (e.g. +5% for excellent presentation). Add a reason so it is visible in the export.
tutorial.grading_step_footer_content: The footer shows the running total, letter grade, and navigation to the next student. Hit Save or Save & Next when you are done.
tutorial.rb_step_meta_content: Give your rubric a name, subject, and grade scale. These appear on every exported report.
tutorial.rb_step_scoring_content: Choose how the final grade is calculated: weighted percentage (most common), total raw points, or single-point mastery.
tutorial.rb_step_criteria_content: Add one criterion per graded dimension. Drag the ⠿ handle to reorder. Each criterion gets performance levels with point ranges.
tutorial.rb_step_save_content: Save your rubric. Then go to Students, pick a student, and click Grade to start scoring.
tutorial.export_step_rubric_content: Select which rubric to export. All PDF, DOCX, and CSV options below apply to this rubric.
tutorial.export_step_template_content: Upload a DOCX template with {{fields}} placeholders. Each student gets a personalised report with their name, score, and feedback merged in.
tutorial.export_step_grades_content: Download all student grades as a formatted PDF or DOCX batch, or a CSV spreadsheet. Tick the students you want first.
tutorial.stats_step_controls_content: Select a rubric and class to load the charts below. Switch the view mode to compare classes or see a single student's history.
tutorial.stats_step_criterion_content: Toggle between a bar chart and a radar chart to see how the class performed on each criterion. Bars near the left reveal which dimensions need more teaching time.
tutorial.cg_step_header_content: Both students appear side by side with their running scores. You compare them directly rather than scoring each in isolation.
tutorial.cg_step_center_content: Track session progress and set how many matchups each student should get. Leave the limit blank for unlimited comparisons.
tutorial.cg_step_compare_content: For each criterion, decide who performed better or mark them equal. The app nudges the scores apart so your grading stays consistent.
tutorial.cg_step_save_content: Save both students and load the next pair. Repeat until every student has been compared enough times.
tutorial.eb_step_prompt_content: Set the title, the essay prompt, and limits like word count, time, and deadline. This is what students see when they open the assignment.
tutorial.eb_step_rubric_content: Connect a rubric so you can grade submissions against it. The rubric is required before you can assign the essay.
tutorial.eb_step_assign_content: Pick a class to assign the essay. Each student gets a personal link and code you can copy and share.
tutorial.eb_step_monitor_content: Paste a submission code to import an essay, or open the live monitor to watch students write in real time.
tutorial.tb_step_details_content: Give the test a name and an optional description. The name is required before you can save.
tutorial.tb_step_settings_content: Set a time limit, choose a grade scale, shuffle questions, or require Safe Exam Browser for locked-down testing.
tutorial.tb_step_sections_content: Group questions into named sections. Drag questions between sections to organise long tests.
tutorial.tb_step_questions_content: Add questions and pick a type per question — multiple choice, open answer, cloze gaps, and more. The total points update automatically.
tutorial.ad_step_grid_content: This grid shows every rubric, test, and essay against each class, with submission counts so you can spot gaps at a glance.
tutorial.ad_step_activity_content: Each row is an activity. Click its name to jump straight to the rubric, test, or essay builder.
tutorial.ad_step_cell_content: Each cell shows progress for that class and lets you link a rubric, assign an essay, or open a test in one click.
tutorial.cefr_step_controls_content: Narrow the view to a single class, or pick a student to drill into their full CEFR profile.
tutorial.cefr_step_view_content: Switch between a class-wide heatmap and a detailed single-student breakdown with skill charts.
tutorial.cefr_step_heatmap_content: Each row is a student and each column is a CEFR level per skill. Green means achieved, amber developing — the outline marks the target level.
tutorial.sp_step_timer_content: Set a duration and run the timer during the speaking task. It can auto-lock the session when time is up.
tutorial.sp_step_pronunciation_content: Tap a button to log a pronunciation slip — word stress, the th-sound, vowels, and more — as you hear it. Each mark adds targeted feedback.
tutorial.sp_step_recordings_content: Record the student speaking for evidence and later review. Recordings can sync to the cloud when configured.
tutorial.sp_step_scoring_content: Score each criterion against the rubric to produce a live grade alongside your pronunciation notes.
tutorial.sprofile_step_header_content: The student's name, class, and track. Print the profile or open the student portal from the top bar.
tutorial.sprofile_step_tabs_content: Switch between the grades overview — average, timeline, and CEFR progress — and the portfolio of past work and speaking sessions.
tutorial.sprofile_step_speaking_content: Launch a timed speaking assessment for this student straight from here, choosing which rubric to use.
tutorial.students_step_roster_content: Pick a class on the left to see its students. Search and click any student to open their full profile.
tutorial.students_step_add_content: Add students one at a time, with name, email, and class. They become available everywhere you grade.
tutorial.students_step_import_content: Bulk-import students from a CSV file, including Magister exports, to set up a whole class at once.

settings.role_teacher_description: Access to teaching tools: grade scales, comment bank, templates, and display settings.
settings.role_student_description: Access to display preferences and language only.

docs.guided_tour_page_tours_prefix: Individual page tours are also available on the Rubric Builder, Statistics, Export, Comparative Grading, Essay Builder, Test Builder, Activity Dashboard, CEFR Overview, Speaking Session, Student Profile, and Students pages via the
docs.route_dashboard_desc: Overview of rubrics, students, recent activity, and quick-start templates. The At-Risk Students panel lets you message a student or assign their recommended grammar practice deck directly from the card.
docs.route_rubrics_desc: Browse, create, import and manage all rubrics.
docs.route_rubrics_new_desc: Create a rubric from scratch or a template.
docs.route_rubric_builder_desc: Edit criteria, levels, CEFR descriptors, standards, and scoring mode.
docs.route_grade_student_desc: Interactive grading with comment bank, voice input, and file attachments.
docs.route_peer_review_desc: Students review each other against the same rubric. The same screen, reached via the "Co-grade" button on Grade Student, lets a colleague act as a second marker.
docs.route_peer_analytics_desc: Consistency scoring of peer reviews against the teacher baseline, feedback heatmaps, and reviewer trends.
docs.route_self_assess_desc: Students self-assess against CEFR Can-Do statements.
docs.route_comparative_grading_desc: Grade two students side-by-side for calibration and consistency.
docs.route_marketplace_desc: Browse, publish, and clone rubrics shared by colleagues at your school. Requires cloud sync and a school.
docs.route_tests_desc: Browse, create, and manage tests and quizzes for assignment to students.
docs.route_tests_new_desc: Create a test from scratch with multiple-choice, multiple-response, true/false, short-answer, open, fill-the-gap, matching, ordering, categorize, and hot text questions.
docs.route_test_builder_desc: Edit test settings (duration, shuffle, Safe Exam Browser requirement, grade scale) and questions, including standards and CEFR linking.
docs.route_test_results_desc: Review a student's submission: auto-scored answers, manual grading for open questions, grade mapping, standards/CEFR breakdowns, and a session integrity panel.
docs.route_test_monitor_desc: Watch a running test in real time: who is online, their progress on each question, and proctoring signals (tab switches, copy/paste, battery, Safe Exam Browser status).
docs.route_essays_desc: Browse, create, and manage essay assignments for your classes.
docs.route_essays_new_desc: Write a prompt, link a rubric, and assign it to students in a class.
docs.route_essay_builder_desc: Edit the prompt, rubric link, and settings; manage assigned students, copy share links, and import submission codes.
docs.route_essay_monitor_desc: Watch students write in real time: presence, live word counts, last-activity timestamps, and an expandable draft preview.
docs.route_students_desc: Manage students and classes, view progress, configure VO tracks.
docs.route_student_profile_desc: Individual grades, CEFR levels, essays, and overdue tracking.
docs.route_student_cefr_overview_desc: Per-student proficiency dashboard across all CEFR skills.
docs.route_learning_path_desc: Rule-based rubric recommendations for CEFR skills where the student trails the class average, plus intervention flags for consecutive low scores.
docs.route_cefr_overview_desc: Whole-class CEFR proficiency dashboard across Reading, Writing, Speaking, Listening.
docs.route_vocabulary_desc: CEFR vocabulary distribution (A1–C2) per class and student, derived from document analysis, with CSV export of vocabulary lists by band.
docs.route_speaking_session_desc: Structured speaking assessments with six CEFR-aligned dimensions.
docs.route_student_portal_desc: Students view grade/progress history, a to-do list of assigned essays and tests, complete self-assessments, and see a read-only note when a grade is under co-grading moderation review and any rule-based learning-path/grammar recommendations. Reachable via a share link, no login required.
docs.route_take_test_desc: Students open a shared link to take a test — answer questions, optional countdown timer, and submit. No login required.
docs.route_feedback_desc: A shared link to one student's grade summary and feedback for a single rubric. No login required.
docs.route_preview_desc: A read-only shared link to a rubric's criteria and levels, with no student data. No login required.
docs.route_essay_take_desc: A student's shared link to write and submit an essay assignment, with autosave and an optional timer. No login required.
docs.route_attachments_desc: Manage uploaded files; OCR and document analysis powered by Tesseract and Mammoth.
docs.route_comments_desc: Reusable feedback snippets organised by tag, insertable during grading.
docs.route_question_bank_desc: Reusable test questions saved outside any one test, insertable into any test.
docs.route_statistics_desc: Grade distributions, per-criterion performance, class comparison, and trend analysis.
docs.route_activity_dashboard_desc: Grid of all rubrics, tests, and essays vs classes — link, assign, and monitor coverage at a glance. Rows can be drag-reordered, and ungraded cells can be assigned to a specific teacher as a grading task.
docs.route_moderation_desc: Queue of co-graded submissions where two teachers disagree above a configurable point threshold — compare per-criterion deltas and keep or accept the second marker's grade.
docs.route_messages_desc: Inbox of student questions about a rubric, test, or essay — reply inline, or start a new thread to a student yourself. Portal-authenticated students only.
docs.route_export_desc: Export graded rubrics as PDF, DOCX (with mail-merge), or CSV.
docs.route_settings_desc: Theme, accent colour, grade scales, language, and backup/restore.
docs.route_admin_desc: User roles, school management, database connection, integrations, data retention.
docs.route_privacy_desc: Full privacy policy for the application.
docs.route_map_intro: All application routes and their parent-child relationships. Routes marked
docs.route_map_intro_middle: are accessible without logging in. Routes marked
docs.gs_quick_start_intro: RubricMaker works entirely offline — all data lives in your browser. No account is needed. You can optionally connect a Supabase database later for multi-device sync.
docs.gs_step1_body_middle: to use the app offline, or
docs.gs_step1_body_suffix: to connect a cloud database. You can always add cloud sync later from Settings.
docs.gs_step2_body_suffix: Pick a quick-start template or start from scratch. Give it a name, subject, and description. Add criteria and levels, then click
docs.gs_step3_body_rest: and fill in their name. Group students into classes by setting the Class field. You can also import from a CSV or a Magister export — the importer auto-detects Dutch column names and, if "Update existing students" is checked, will update matching records instead of creating duplicates. Moving a student to a new class (manually, or via a re-import that detects a class transfer) keeps a "Past classes" trail on their profile — none of their grading history is lost. When "Update existing students" is on, you'll see a confirmation summarizing how many students will transfer class and how many will be archived before anything is applied — useful for promoting a whole class to the next year.
docs.gs_step4_body_prefix: Open any rubric from the Rubrics page. Click the student's name to open the grading view. Click each criterion level, add comments, attach files, and click
docs.gs_step5_body: Each student has a unique portal link (shown in their profile). Share it so they can view grades, submit essays, and complete self-assessments — no login required on their end.
docs.gs_guided_tour_body: A built-in Joyride walkthrough runs the first time you log in. You can restart it at any time from
docs.gs_mode_offline_desc: All data in browser localStorage. Nothing sent to a server. Works on any static host.
docs.gs_mode_cloud_desc: Connect a Supabase project (self-hosted or supabase.com) for multi-device access and sharing.
docs.gs_mode_docker_desc: docker-compose up spins up the frontend + Supabase + Caddy (HTTPS). Recommended for schools.
docs.gs_themes_intro_prefix: Personalise the look of the app and your exported rubrics from
docs.gs_themes_item_accent: Accent color — pick a custom hex color or one of 8 preset swatches.
docs.gs_themes_item_font: Interface font — choose the typeface used throughout the app (Inter, Nunito, Source Sans 3, Lato, Roboto).
docs.gs_themes_item_bundles: Theme bundles — Academy, Nature, Midnight, Warm, Slate, and Rose apply a matching accent color, interface font, and rubric header color in one click.
docs.gs_themes_item_export_font: Rubric export font — in the Rubric Builder formatting panel, choose the font used for headings in PDF and Word exports, including decorative options like Playfair Display, Oswald, Bebas Neue, Special Elite, and Courier Prime.
docs.gs_themes_item_dyslexia: Dyslexia-friendly reading mode — a Settings toggle that increases line spacing and letter spacing app-wide to improve readability for dyslexic readers.
docs.gs_classes_intro: Each class on the Students page can carry a school year and a Dutch VO track, used across cohort filters, CEFR target levels, and rubric differentiation.
docs.gs_classes_item_year: School year — groep 7/8 (primary) or jaar 1–6 (secondary), set from a class's settings.
docs.gs_classes_item_track: VO track — VMBO-BB/KB/TL, HAVO, or VWO; only shown for jaar 1–6 classes.
docs.gs_classes_item_student_track: Per-student track override — a student can differ from their class's default track by at most one level (e.g. VMBO-TL/HAVO but not VMBO-BB/VWO), set when editing that student.
docs.gs_classes_item_color: Custom class color — pick a color in a class's settings for faster visual scanning; falls back to the track's default color when unset.
docs.gs_install_body: RubricMaker can be installed to your device's home screen or desktop for a faster, app-like launch with no browser chrome — look for an install icon in your browser's address bar, or the "Install" option in your browser's menu. It keeps working fully offline, exactly like the browser version.
docs.rb_builder_intro: The Rubric Builder is the core of RubricMaker. Open any rubric from the Rubrics list, or create a new one, to enter the builder.
docs.rb_builder_views_suffix: at any time using the toggle in the toolbar.
docs.rb_scoring_modes_item_total: Total Points — raw score from all criteria levels.
docs.rb_scoring_modes_item_weighted: Weighted Percentage — each criterion has a weight (%). Final score is the weighted average.
docs.rb_scoring_modes_item_single: Single-Point Rubric — one level per criterion (meets / does not meet); pass/fail logic.
docs.rb_level_options_item_checklist: Sub-item checklists within a level (students must complete specific items).
docs.rb_level_options_item_ranges: Point ranges — set a min and max; teacher picks a value inside the range during grading.
docs.rb_level_options_item_modifiers: Score modifiers — apply %, point, or level adjustments with an optional reason.
docs.rb_standards_item_cefr: CEFR Can-Do descriptors — attach specific descriptors per criterion.
docs.rb_standards_item_ib: IB Learner Profile attributes and Bloom's Taxonomy levels.
docs.rb_standards_item_ccss: CCSS, NGSS, and state standards via the Common Standards Project API.
docs.rb_standards_item_grammar: Grammar linker — open the alignment picker and switch to the Grammar tab. Each grammar topic is a header (e.g. Past Simple) and its distinctions (regular verbs, irregular verbs) are separate, individually linkable standards. Filter by CEFR level (levels follow the CEFR-J Grammar Profile). Linked grammar can be auto-checked in scanned essays during grading — see Grading.
docs.rb_version_history_body_prefix: Every save creates an automatic snapshot. Click
docs.rb_version_history_body_mid: in the toolbar to browse past versions and restore any of them. Manual labels can be added before saving. Click
docs.rb_version_history_body_suffix: next to any version to see exactly what changed between that version and the current rubric — added/removed criteria, title and weight changes, and level point changes.
docs.rb_save_template_body_mid: button to store the current rubric as a reusable template. Your saved templates appear under
docs.rb_save_template_body_suffix: when creating a new rubric on the Dashboard.
docs.rb_import_export_item_import: Import: upload a JSON file exported from another RubricMaker instance, or paste a share code.
docs.rb_import_export_item_export: Export: PDF rubric document, DOCX, or raw JSON for backup.
docs.rb_import_export_item_share_code: Share code: a compact base64 code you can send to a colleague who can import it in one click.
docs.rb_marketplace_body_suffix: page lets you publish a rubric for colleagues at your school to browse and clone, and upvote the ones you find useful. It requires cloud sync to be configured and your account to belong to a school — without both, the page shows a short explanation instead of the listings. Cloning copies a listing into your own rubrics without affecting the original.
docs.rb_marketplace_cefr_tags_body: When publishing, you can tag the rubric with the CEFR level(s) it targets so colleagues can spot it at a glance.
docs.rb_marketplace_kinds_note: Tests and flashcard decks can be published to the same marketplace from the Tests and Flashcards pages, filterable by type.
docs.rb_department_sharing_body_prefix: Unlike the Marketplace (publish a copy for others to clone), the share dialog on the Rubrics list has a
docs.rb_department_sharing_body_suffix: checkbox that makes the live rubric read-only visible to every colleague in your school — edits you make keep flowing through automatically. Requires cloud sync and a school. Rubrics shared this way by colleagues appear in a "Shared with your department" section on the Rubrics list.
docs.rb_reorder_body: Drag the grip handle on a rubric card to reorder the Rubrics list. The same drag-to-reorder works on the Tests list, the Essays list, and each section of the Activity Dashboard. Order is per-teacher and persists across reloads.
docs.rb_tests_intro: Build tests and quizzes from the
docs.rb_tests_intro_body: page. Add multiple-choice, multiple-response (select all that apply), true/false, short-answer, open, fill-the-gap, matching, ordering, categorize, and hot text questions, set a duration, due date, and grade scale, and optionally require Safe Exam Browser. Link standards and CEFR descriptors per question, then assign the test to a class — each student gets a unique share link to complete it. The due date is shown in the test list and defaults into the assignment share link's deadline, so you don't have to re-enter it each time. Every question type has a small
docs.rb_tests_intro_suffix: help button for both you and your students explaining how to author and answer it.
docs.rb_tests_fillgap_markers: markers in the question text. Write
docs.rb_tests_fillgap_blank: to create a blank that students type into — add alternative correct spellings separated by a pipe, e.g.
docs.rb_tests_fillgap_dropdown_intro: For a dropdown instead of free text, choose the
docs.rb_tests_fillgap_dropdown_list: type and list the correct answer first, e.g.
docs.rb_tests_fillgap_body_suffix: — students see the options in a shuffled order. The builder's "Insert gap" / "Insert dropdown gap" buttons add the markers for you, and a preview shows the parsed gaps and their correct answers.
docs.rb_tests_matching_body: let students work with several related items at once. For
docs.rb_tests_matching_body2: , enter left/right pairs — students see the left items in order and pick a matching right item from a dropdown for each. For
docs.rb_tests_ordering_body: , enter the items in their correct order using the up/down arrows — students see them shuffled and reorder them with arrows. For
docs.rb_tests_categorize_body: , define categories and assign each item to the category it belongs to — students sort each item into a category via a dropdown. All three support an
docs.rb_tests_partial_credit_body: toggle, which awards points proportionally for each correct pair, position, or item instead of all-or-nothing.
docs.rb_tests_hottext_body: ask students to click selectable words or phrases within a passage. Write the passage and wrap each selectable fragment in double square brackets, e.g.
docs.rb_tests_hottext_body2: — the "Insert selectable fragment" button wraps your current text selection for you. Below the passage, use the checkmarks to mark which fragments students should select. Students click highlighted fragments to toggle them on or off.
docs.rb_tests_hottext_body4: awards points per fragment instead of all-or-nothing.
docs.rb_tests_due_date_body: Set a due date in the test builder to show an overdue badge in the test list once it passes, and to default the expiry date when generating student share links.
docs.rb_tests_student_view_item_seb: If the test requires Safe Exam Browser, students see a blocking screen until they open the link inside SEB.
docs.rb_tests_student_view_item_timer: A countdown timer (when set) shows time remaining and auto-submits the test when it reaches zero.
docs.rb_tests_student_view_item_autosave: Answers are autosaved to the device as the student works, so a reload or browser crash restores their progress.
docs.rb_tests_student_view_item_submit: Submitting produces a confirmation and, when no cloud sync is configured, a submission code for the student to send to their teacher.
docs.rb_live_monitor_intro_prefix: While a test or essay is in progress, click
docs.rb_live_monitor_intro_suffix: (from the Tests page for a test, or from the assignment dialog for an essay) to open a live view of the session. This requires cloud sync to be enabled — without it, the page explains that you can review progress after the fact from each student's saved answers or draft instead.
docs.rb_live_monitor_item_presence: Presence — a coloured dot shows whether each student is active, idle, or disconnected, based on how recently their browser checked in.
docs.rb_live_monitor_item_grid: Response grid (tests) — a students-by-questions matrix, colour-coded as students answer. Click a question header to open a gallery of every answer to that question.
docs.rb_live_monitor_item_draft: Live draft preview (essays) — a card per student with a live word count, last-activity timestamp, and an expandable preview of what they are currently writing.
docs.rb_live_monitor_item_hide_names: Hide names — toggle to anonymise the view, useful when sharing your screen.
docs.rb_live_monitor_item_proctoring: Proctoring flags — counts of tab switches, copy/paste/cut, battery level, and Safe Exam Browser status. These are advisory signals only: a determined student can spoof them, so use them to prompt a conversation, not as definitive proof.
docs.rb_live_monitor_item_nudge: Nudge — sends a short check-in message to one student's screen via a live toast notification.
docs.gr_interface_intro_prefix: Open a rubric from the Rubrics list, then click a student's name to enter the grading view. Use
docs.gr_interface_intro_suffix: to move through your class efficiently.
docs.gr_scoring_item_select: Click any level cell to select it. Click again to deselect.
docs.gr_scoring_item_slider: For point ranges, use the slider to choose a value within the allowed range.
docs.gr_scoring_item_subitems: Toggle sub-items inside a level with checkboxes.
docs.gr_scoring_item_modifiers: Apply score modifiers (% bonus, point deduction, level adjustment) with a reason.
docs.gr_mobile_body_prefix: On phones and iPad portrait the grading view is touch-optimised. Point-range and sub-item sliders gain a
docs.gr_mobile_body_suffix: stepper for precise scoring without dragging a thin slider, the level cards and score pickers use larger tap targets, and the Save bar stays pinned to the bottom of the screen. Comparative grading stacks into a single column so each student's panel is full-width.
docs.gr_feedback_item_comment_bank: Comment Bank — insert reusable snippets by tag. Build the bank from the Comment Bank page.
docs.gr_feedback_item_voice: Voice grading — dictate comments hands-free using the browser's speech recognition API.
docs.gr_feedback_item_notes: Per-criterion notes in addition to the overall feedback field.
docs.gr_feedback_item_attachments: File attachments — upload evidence files (images, PDFs) per graded rubric.
docs.gr_comparative_body_suffix: on the Rubrics list to open the side-by-side grading view. Grade two students at once for calibration and anchor-based grading. The interface adaptively suggests level adjustments when there is a mismatch between students' scores.
docs.gr_group_body_suffix: on the Rubrics list to pick two or more students who will share one grade — useful for group projects. You're taken to the normal single-student grading screen; saving any one member's grade copies its scores and comments to the rest of the group, including on later edits. Each student still has their own record (so feedback can mention them by name), but the scores stay in sync across the whole group. In the Rubric Builder, uncheck "Group grading" on a specific criterion to keep it individually scored per student even during group grading.
docs.gr_delete_body: The trash icon on a student's grading page removes that grade (with a confirmation first). For a group-graded submission you're asked whether to remove just that student's copy or the whole group's shared grade. Deleted grades aren't gone for good — a teacher/admin can restore them from Admin → Archive → Recently deleted grades.
docs.gr_peer_item_peer_review: Peer Review — share a URL with a student to review a classmate's work.
docs.gr_peer_item_self_assess: Self-Assessment — students rate themselves against CEFR Can-Do statements. Results are stored alongside teacher scores.
docs.gr_peer_item_analytics: Peer Review Analytics (/peer-analytics/:rubricId) — open from the Peer Review screen to see consistency scores comparing peer grades to your baseline, a feedback heatmap of which criteria get the most comments, and round-over-round trends. Reviews are matched to reviewers via their student ID; older records without one are shown as "Anonymous reviewer".
docs.gr_cograding_body_mid: on a graded student's page to send the submission for an independent second marking — it opens the same screen as peer review, just with a colleague instead of a classmate as the reviewer. With cloud sync connected and a school set up, pick the colleague from a dropdown populated from your school's member list, so the same colleague is always recognised as the same reviewer even if their name is typed differently elsewhere. Without cloud sync (or no school yet), the dialog falls back to a free-text name/email field. Any submission where the two grades disagree by more than a configurable point threshold shows up in
docs.gr_cograding_body_suffix: in the sidebar, with a per-criterion delta breakdown and a one-click choice to keep the original grade or accept the second marker's.
docs.gr_cograding_reconcile_body_suffix: merges the two grades by averaging the points on each diverging criterion, instead of picking one marker's grade outright.
docs.gr_cograding_pending_note: The sidebar's Moderation link shows a count of pending disputes, and each queue item shows how many days it's been waiting — oldest first.
docs.gr_cograding_digest_note: Self-hosted deployments can opt into a nightly email reminder about pending disputes — enable "Send me a nightly email digest" under Settings → Email Digest (requires the scheduled-digest server job to be set up, see docs/SELF_HOSTING_OPS.md).
docs.gr_messages_body_prefix: Every question a student asks from their portal about a rubric, test, or essay lands in your
docs.gr_messages_body_suffix: inbox as an unread notification. Reply inline, or start a new thread to a student yourself — pick them from a list and write a note.
docs.gr_messages_scope_note: Only students who log into the student portal can message you; a bare share-code link (test/essay hand-in with no portal account) doesn't have a place to receive a reply.
docs.gr_comment_bank_body: in the sidebar to manage your snippets. Organise them with tags (e.g. "writing", "grammar", "effort"). During grading, click the comment icon to open the bank and insert any snippet into the feedback field with one click. With cloud sync connected, click the department icon on an item to share it read-only with every other teacher in your school; items shared by colleagues appear in the bank with a "Department" badge.
docs.gr_comment_bank_suggested_note: If a criterion has had a run of low scores, the comment bank opens with a "Suggested" group at the top — comments tagged with that criterion's CEFR skill or level (e.g. "speaking_interaction", "B1").
docs.gr_question_bank_body_prefix: Save any test question to a reusable bank via the bookmark icon in the question editor, then insert saved questions into any test from
docs.gr_question_bank_body_suffix: — manage, tag, and search saved questions there.
docs.gr_test_results_intro_suffix: on a test to see every student's submission and open results at
docs.gr_test_results_item_autoscore: Multiple-choice and exact-match short-answer questions are auto-scored and marked correct/incorrect. Short-answer questions can accept several correct wordings by separating them with | in the expected answer field.
docs.gr_test_results_item_open: Open questions (and short-answer questions without a model answer) get a manual points input — clamped to the question's max — plus a feedback field.
docs.gr_test_results_item_grade: Total points, percentage, and a letter/scale grade (from the test's grade scale) are shown at the top of the page.
docs.gr_test_results_item_standards: Standards and CEFR breakdowns roll up points earned per linked standard or Can-Do descriptor, mirroring the Learning Goals view.
docs.gr_test_results_item_integrity: A "Session integrity" panel summarises proctoring events (tab switches, copy/paste, battery, SEB status) captured while the student took the test, plus how long they spent on it compared to the class average — flagged if it looks unusually fast.
docs.gr_test_results_item_audio_response: Audio-response questions let students record a spoken answer directly in the browser; play it back and grade it manually, same as an open question.
docs.gr_test_mode_body: In the test settings panel, set Mode to Practice to make a test ungraded and formative, or leave it as Assessment for the normal graded, due-dated flow.
docs.gr_test_mode_item_practice: Practice-mode results never affect a student's graded CEFR progress chart — they show separately as practice progress instead.
docs.gr_test_mode_item_cefr: Tag a test with a target CEFR level and skill (like rubrics already support) so its results can feed the student's CEFR overview.
docs.gr_test_mode_item_audio: Add an audio URL to any question for listening comprehension — students see an audio player above the answer area, the same way question images already work.
docs.gr_test_mode_item_retake: Enable "Allow students to retake this test" on a practice-mode test so students can attempt it more than once.
docs.gr_test_mode_item_grammar: Set the practice category to Grammar and tag cloze, hot-text, or matching questions with a specific grammar item so the question feeds a student's grammar practice recommendations.
docs.gr_test_rich_authoring_body: Question prompts and section passages use the same rich-text editor as essays — bold, tables, sub/superscript, links, and more.
docs.gr_test_rich_authoring_item_prompt: Format any question prompt with bold, tables, sub/superscript, links, and colour — useful for math, chemistry, or other notation.
docs.gr_test_rich_authoring_item_passage: Add a reading passage or stimulus to a section (via its "Passage / stimulus" toggle) — shown to students above that section's questions.
docs.gr_test_rich_authoring_item_option_image: Attach an image to any individual multiple-choice or multiple-response option, for picture-choice questions.
docs.gr_test_rich_authoring_item_cloze_gap: Insert fill-in-the-gap blanks with a click instead of typing {{...}} syntax — click a gap pill to edit its accepted answers.
docs.gr_placement_body: Set Mode to Placement to quickly estimate an incoming student's CEFR level — useful at the start of the year, where a single fixed test is either too hard or too easy for some students. Choose a Placement engine: Multistage (teacher-authored branching sections) or Staircase (per-question adaptive ladder).
docs.gr_placement_item_routing: Multistage engine: in the Sections panel, tag each section with a target CEFR level and set up routing — a pass threshold plus which section to send the student to next depending on their score in that section.
docs.gr_placement_item_staircase: Staircase engine: tag each section with a CEFR level instead — it becomes a question pool for that level, drawn from the test's own CEFR-tagged sections. Every student starts at A2, moves up after two correct answers in a row, moves down after a miss, and the test ends once the level settles.
docs.gr_placement_item_taking: Multistage: students see one section at a time, with the routing rules deciding which section comes next. Staircase: students answer one question at a time with no going back, each answer immediately deciding the next question's level.
docs.gr_placement_item_results: Test results show the exact path the student took through the sections and their score in each one, rather than a single whole-test percentage.
docs.gr_placement_item_provisional: The outcome is a provisional CEFR estimate, clearly badged as such wherever it appears — it feeds the student's CEFR overview and learning path until a real graded assessment replaces it, but is never shown as an achieved level.
docs.gr_class_avg_body_suffix: on a test to see the Class average adjustment tool. Enter a target average percentage, preview how each student's points and percentage would change with a uniform point adjustment, then
docs.gr_class_avg_body2: . Raw answer scores are never overwritten — the adjustment is stored separately and is fully reversible with
docs.gr_class_avg_body3: , and the audit trail records when it was applied.
docs.gr_item_analysis_body: From the test list, expand a test's results to see per-question item analysis: a discrimination index (do high scorers get this item right more often than low scorers?) and the most commonly chosen wrong option for multiple-choice/multiple-response questions — useful for spotting broken questions or common misconceptions.
docs.gr_import_offline_body_prefix: If a student submits a test as a code instead of online (e.g. when offline), click
docs.gr_import_offline_body_suffix: on the Tests list, paste the code, and the submission is created or updated automatically — ready for grading and results.
docs.gr_test_summary_body_suffix: on a test to find the
docs.gr_test_summary_body2: panel. Choose a single student or the whole class, then export a PDF or Word summary with per-question accuracy and a strong/developing/weak breakdown by linked standard or CEFR descriptor — useful for spotting which topics a student or the class as a whole needs to revisit.
docs.gr_portal_intro_prefix: Each student gets a personal dashboard with their grade history, CEFR progress, and feedback. Share it via the
docs.gr_portal_intro_suffix: button on the Students page, or let the student log in themselves (email OTP or a teacher-set password) — it's also reachable directly at
docs.gr_portal_work_body: A combined to-do list of every assigned essay and test, grouped into Overdue, Planned, and Completed. Each item shows its status (not started, in progress, submitted) and due date where the teacher set one. Tests open in one click from the list — no need to hunt down a share-code link.
docs.gr_portal_progress_body: A radar chart of the student's own per-criterion scores, either combined across every rubric they've been graded on (criteria with the same name are averaged together) or filtered to a single rubric — shown once they have at least 3 distinct graded criteria.
docs.gr_portal_messages_body: A student can ask a question about a specific rubric grade, test, or essay — or a general question not tied to any of them — right from their portal, and see the teacher's reply on their next visit.
docs.ce_overview_intro: The CEFR (Common European Framework of Reference) module tracks language proficiency across Reading, Writing, Speaking, and Listening — aligned to Dutch VO targets (VMBO-BB through VWO).
docs.ce_overview_item_whole_class: Whole-class overview (/cefr-overview) — heatmap of all students across CEFR levels per skill.
docs.ce_overview_item_per_student: Per-student overview — timeline of assessed levels, self-assessment scores alongside teacher scores.
docs.ce_overview_item_progress: Progress tracking — see which students are on-track, ahead, or behind the expected CEFR range for their specific school year and VO track (not just a single track-wide target).
docs.ce_overview_item_evidence: Skill evidence — opening a student's detail view shows, per skill and level, which assessments contributed and why it's marked achieved or developing.
docs.ce_overview_info: CEFR levels are computed from rubric scores when CEFR descriptors are linked to criteria. They are also updated by speaking sessions and self-assessments.
docs.ce_mastery_targets_intro: Set an expected mastery percentage for a linked standard (CCSS, NGSS, Dutch kerndoelen, or custom) at a given school year and VO track — configured once per standard from Settings → Teaching, reused everywhere that standard is linked across your rubrics.
docs.ce_mastery_targets_item_config: Configuration — add, edit, or remove targets from Settings → Teaching → Standard mastery targets.
docs.ce_mastery_targets_item_status: Status display — a student's or class's learning-goal progress (student profile, class Statistics view) shows ahead, on track, or behind once a matching target is set.
docs.ce_learning_paths_intro: Open a student's profile and click "Learning Path" to see rule-based, offline suggestions — no AI is involved, only deterministic comparisons against class averages and score history.
docs.ce_learning_paths_item_recs: Rubric recommendations — for each CEFR skill/level where the student scores well below the class average, untried rubrics tagged with that skill and level are suggested.
docs.ce_learning_paths_item_flags: Intervention flags — three or more consecutive low scores on the same rubric criterion, or on the same CEFR skill, raise a flag so it is easy to spot students who need extra support.
docs.ce_learning_paths_item_computed: Everything is computed on the fly from existing grades; nothing new is stored.
docs.ce_learning_paths_item_grammar: A separate Grammar practice section suggests a matching flashcard deck and practice test when a student repeatedly scores poorly on a grammar-linked criterion or question.
docs.ce_mastery_profile_body: On a student's profile page, a table merges test accuracy, flashcard spaced-repetition progress, and graded-writing scores per grammar item — a single view of what a student has actually demonstrated across every domain that tags the same grammar point.
docs.ce_speaking_intro: Start a speaking session from either of two entry points:
docs.ce_speaking_item_profile: Student Profile → "Speaking Session" button in the top bar. With one rubric it navigates directly; with multiple rubrics a dropdown lets you pick.
docs.ce_speaking_item_rubrics: Rubrics → find a rubric card → "Speaking…" dropdown at the bottom — select a student to jump straight into the session.
docs.ce_speaking_item_past: Past sessions appear in a "Speaking Sessions" card on the Student Profile, each with a link to reopen and extend it.
docs.ce_speaking_item_inside: Inside the session: set a timer, mark pronunciation quick-marks (word stress, th-sound, etc.), score the linked rubric criteria, and add an overall comment.
docs.ce_speaking_item_results: Results are saved to the student's CEFR history and are visible on the CEFR Overview heatmap.
docs.ce_speaking_item_recording: Record audio (and, with cloud sync enabled, video) directly inside the session — recordings are saved with the session and play back from the "Portfolio" tab on the Student Profile.
docs.ce_speaking_item_practice_mode: Toggle Graded/Practice before recording — Practice is ungraded and retakeable as many times as needed, kept separate from the graded attempt.
docs.ce_speaking_info: Recordings are privacy-first: without cloud sync configured, audio recording is available but files stay only in this browser's storage (cleared if the user clears browsing data); video recording is disabled in that case. With cloud sync enabled, both audio and video recordings sync to the teacher's private storage. A single recording is capped at 50MB.
docs.ce_self_assess_body: Students access self-assessment via their portal link or a direct URL. They rate themselves against the Can-Do descriptors attached to a rubric's criteria. Their ratings and reflection text are stored alongside the teacher's scores and are visible on the Student Profile and CEFR Overview pages.
docs.ce_cambridge_intro: Enable "Show Cambridge exam labels" in Settings → General to display the Cambridge English Qualification that maps to each CEFR level (A2 Key, B1 Preliminary, B2 First, C1 Advanced, C2 Proficiency) as a subtle secondary label next to CEFR badges on the Student CEFR Overview and Student Profile pages. A1 has no main-suite Cambridge exam, so no label is shown for it.
docs.ce_cambridge_item_lookup: Rubric Builder vocabulary panel — when a Cambridge Dictionary API key is set in Admin → Integrations, each word gets a "Look up" button that fetches its CEFR level and definition.
docs.ce_cambridge_item_fill: Lookups only fill in empty CEFR level / definition fields and never overwrite values you already entered.
docs.ce_cambridge_item_offline: Without an API key, the lookup button is hidden and the app continues to work fully offline using the bundled CEFR-J vocabulary data.
docs.es_workspace_intro_suffix: section in the sidebar is the dedicated home for essay assignments — separate from the Rubrics page. It lists every essay you have created, grouped by assignment, with the linked rubric, the number of assigned students, and how many have submitted.
docs.es_creating_item_new: Click "New Essay" to open the builder: write the title and prompt, set optional word count limits (min/max), a time limit, and a deadline.
docs.es_creating_item_rubric: Use the rubric connector to link a rubric for structured feedback after grading.
docs.es_creating_item_readonly: Enable "Read-only after submit" to lock essays once submitted.
docs.es_creating_item_seb: Enable "Require Safe Exam Browser (SEB)" to lock the assignment to the SEB app — download the .seb config from the assignment dialog to distribute to students.
docs.es_creating_item_assign: Click "Assign to students" to pick a class — each selected student gets their own share link and submission code.
docs.es_creating_item_status: Once assigned, the builder lists every student with their submission status and a per-student "Copy link" button.
docs.es_creating_item_import: Use "Import submission code" to add a student's completed essay (pasted code) when no database connection is configured.
docs.es_creating_item_monitor: Click "Monitor" to open the live essay monitor for the assignment.
docs.es_creating_item_save_config: Save configuration — click "Save configuration" in the assignment dialog footer to store the title, prompt, limits, and deadline locally for this rubric. The form auto-fills from the saved configuration the next time you open the assignment dialog, so you can prepare assignments in advance and only share links when you are ready.
docs.es_editor_body_prefix: Students write in a TipTap-powered editor with a full formatting toolbar: bold, italic, headings, lists, tables, text colour, highlight, superscript, and more. Toggle
docs.es_editor_body_suffix: in the toolbar to switch to an A4-sized paper layout with realistic margins — ideal for essay writing practice.
docs.es_doc_analysis_item_upload: Upload a DOCX or PDF to import text into the editor.
docs.es_doc_analysis_item_ocr: OCR via Tesseract.js for scanned images or PDFs.
docs.es_doc_analysis_item_grammar_check: Vocabulary and grammar checking via LanguageTool integration.
docs.es_doc_analysis_item_grammar_qual: Grammar qualification — if a rubric criterion has linked grammar standards (see Rubric Builder → Grammar tab), scanning the essay reports per-criterion which structures the student demonstrated (✔), which were not found (✘), and which need a manual check (⦸). Detection is approximate, rule/NLP-based (no AI), using the CEFR-J Grammar Profile. Click "Apply as comment" to append the summary to that criterion's feedback.
docs.es_submission_body1: Students visit the essay URL or enter the submission code in the Student Portal. After an optional email verification gate, they write and submit. Drafts are auto-saved to localStorage every few seconds. After submission, teachers open the linked rubric's grading view and click
docs.es_submission_body2: to pull the submission from the database (or paste the student's submission code if no database is connected) — the essay text is then added as an attachment, visible in both the normal and comparative grading views.
docs.es_submission_body3_prefix: If an assignment requires Safe Exam Browser and the student opens the link outside of SEB, submission is blocked and a banner offers a
docs.es_submission_body3_suffix: button so the student can get the right config file and reopen the assignment inside SEB.
docs.es_export_intro_suffix: page, pick an essay assignment to export student submissions as Markdown, DOCX, or PDF.
docs.es_export_item_formats: Export a single essay, or select several students at once
docs.es_export_item_batch: Download selected essays as separate files, or combine them into one document (DOCX page breaks / PDF page breaks per student)
docs.es_export_item_combined: Optionally include the student's rubric grade and grammar/vocabulary analysis alongside the essay (DOCX or PDF only)
docs.es_export_item_ics: Export every essay assignment's deadline as a downloadable .ics calendar file
docs.es_export_item_styling: Text color, highlights, fonts, alignment, tables, and checklists from the editor are preserved in DOCX and Markdown exports, not just PDF
docs.an_portfolio_body_suffix: tab shows a chronological timeline that combines rubric grades, speaking sessions, and self-assessments in one scrollable view. Each entry shows the date, type, rubric name, and score with a direct link to view or edit the full record.
docs.an_stats_intro_suffix: in the sidebar. Filter by class, track, and year. Three views:
docs.an_stats_item_by_rubric: By Rubric — grade distribution, per-criterion averages, score histogram, class trend.
docs.an_stats_item_by_student: By Student — per-student rubric history and criterion radar comparison.
docs.an_stats_item_compare: Compare — select up to 4 classes, compare averages, criterion gaps, and trend lines side by side. Collapsible Insights panel flags struggling classes, weak criteria, and divergence between classes.
docs.an_stats_item_filters: Track and year filter dropdowns scope the class selector to Dutch VO tracks (VMBO/HAVO/VWO) and school year.
docs.an_activity_intro_suffix: in the sidebar. Rows are rubrics, tests, and essays; columns are classes. Each cell shows how many students submitted/were graded and a quick action:
docs.an_activity_item_rubrics: Rubrics — Link/Unlink adds or removes the rubric from a class's assignment list. When a cell has ungraded students, an Assign button lets you hand that batch to a specific colleague as a grading task.
docs.an_activity_item_essays: Essays — Assign All bulk-creates essay assignments for all unenrolled students in the class.
docs.an_activity_item_tests: Tests — Open navigates to the test builder where you can share the class link.
docs.an_activity_item_filter: Filter by school year and VO track to narrow the column set.
docs.an_activity_item_coverage: Standards Coverage panel (below the grid) — pick a class to see every standard linked anywhere in its rubrics, split into "Assessed" (with average score) and "Not yet assessed" — the gap to address before the term ends.
docs.an_activity_item_reorder: Drag the grip handle on any row to reorder rubrics, tests, or essays within their section.
docs.an_activity_pending: Pending grading tasks (assigned but not yet graded) are listed above the grid, with the assignee's name and an optional due date. A task automatically disappears once a grade is saved for that student and rubric — there's nothing to mark "done" manually.
docs.an_export_intro_suffix: in the sidebar. Choose your format and scope.
docs.an_export_item_pdf: PDF — individual student reports or bulk class export in one PDF.
docs.an_export_item_docx: DOCX — raw export or mail-merge template with field substitution ({{student_name}}, {{score}}, etc.).
docs.an_export_item_csv: CSV — raw grade data for Excel or your school's gradebook.
docs.an_export_item_csv_presets: A gradebook format dropdown next to the CSV button switches between the generic export and ready-made column presets for Magister and SOMtoday (grades converted to the Dutch 1-10 scale).
docs.an_export_item_period: Period report — aggregated CEFR progress report for a class over a date range.
docs.an_export_item_report_cards: Report cards — a single consolidated DOCX per student, combining rubric grades, standards coverage, learning goals, and CEFR overview into one document.
docs.an_export_info: Mail-merge DOCX templates support custom fields. Upload a .docx file with placeholder fields and the app will substitute them with student data.
docs.an_report_cards_intro: On the Export page, below Period Report, use the Report Card panel to generate a consolidated DOCX per student. It reuses the same class and student picker, date range, and period label as the Period Report above.
docs.an_report_cards_item_toggle: Toggle which sections appear: rubric grades, standards coverage, learning goals, CEFR overview, and test summary.
docs.an_report_cards_item_export: Export a single student’s report card, or batch-export one DOCX per selected student.
docs.an_report_cards_item_summary: Test summary combines every test the student took within the selected date range, showing per-skill strong/weak points.
docs.an_report_cards_item_chart: A grade trend chart image is included alongside the grades table when at least two rubrics are graded in the period.
docs.an_vocab_intro_prefix: Go to Vocabulary in the sidebar (
docs.an_vocab_intro_suffix: ). This dashboard aggregates CEFR vocabulary levels (A1–C2) detected in students' analysed documents.
docs.an_vocab_item_chart: Per-class stacked bar chart of vocabulary level distribution, with a class selector.
docs.an_vocab_item_drilldown: Per-student drill-down showing each student’s estimated vocabulary level and profiled word count.
docs.an_vocab_item_csv: CSV export of vocabulary words — filter by a single CEFR band (A1–C2) or export all levels — with word, level, definition, and source columns.
docs.an_vocab_info: This dashboard is read-only and derives entirely from existing document analysis results (see Attachments → Analyse) and rubric vocabulary lists — analyse student submissions first to populate it.
docs.da_management_intro: All data lives in localStorage by default. Supabase sync is optional.
docs.da_backup_item_export: Export entire dataset to a single JSON file from Settings.
docs.da_backup_item_restore: Restore from any prior backup JSON — replaces all current data.
docs.da_backup_item_docker: Docker deployments: use scripts/backup.sh and scripts/restore.sh for server-side backups.
docs.da_sync_item_env: Set VITE_SUPABASE_URL and VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY to enable sync.
docs.da_sync_item_source: LocalStorage stays the source of truth; Supabase is a sync layer.
docs.da_sync_item_collab: Multi-teacher collaboration: share rubrics between accounts in the same Supabase project.
docs.da_sync_item_otp: Email OTP login — optional; requires SMTP configuration in Docker.
docs.da_sync_item_student_password: Student password login — from the Students page, generate a password for a student with an email on file, then share it with them directly. They sign in at the student portal with their email and that password, bypassing OTP email entirely (useful when school email filters block or delay Supabase's OTP messages).
docs.da_sync_item_conflict: Conflict resolution: when local and cloud data differ, the newest change wins. Edits made while offline are protected and never overwritten by older cloud data until they have synced.
docs.da_stress_body_mid: to record diagnostic events (actions, sync results, errors — never essay text, comments, or grades) to a
docs.da_stress_body_suffix: table during a pilot. Useful for running a full-class test before rollout. Disable by rebuilding without the flag once the pilot is done.
docs.da_admin_intro_suffix: for users with the admin role.
docs.da_admin_item_users: Users tab — assign roles (admin, teacher, student), invite colleagues.
docs.da_admin_item_schools: Schools tab — manage school names and branding.
docs.da_admin_item_database: Database tab — configure Supabase connection, push/pull data, manage sharing.
docs.da_admin_item_integrations: Integrations tab — Standards API key, Cambridge Dictionary API key.
docs.da_admin_item_data: Data tab — anonymise student data, bulk delete, compliance tools.
docs.da_admin_item_retention: Retention tab — set automatic data retention policies per school.
docs.da_admin_item_audit: Audit Log tab — filterable, paginated log of role changes, grade saves, exports, and auth events; admins see all entries, teachers see their own. Entries are retained for 3 years (admin), 1 year (grade), 1 month (export/auth) then automatically purged.
docs.da_settings_item_theme: Theme — dark / light, accent colour picker, 8 quick presets, or choose a named theme bundle (Academy, Nature, Midnight, Warm, Slate, Rose) that sets font, accent, and export colour in one click.
docs.da_settings_item_scales: Grade scales — create custom scales (1–10, A–F, etc.) and set a default.
docs.da_settings_item_lang: Language — English, Dutch, French, German, Spanish.
docs.da_settings_item_tour: Guided tour — restart the onboarding walkthrough.
docs.da_settings_item_backup: Backup / restore — download or upload the full dataset JSON.
docs.da_settings_item_export_templates: Export templates — upload a blank .docx to style exports: a rubric table template (column headers/colour) or an essay/period-report style template (heading and body font, extracted from the file's own styles). Set one of each as the active default.
docs.gs_search_intro_suffix: from anywhere in the app (or click the search icon in the Topbar) to open a quick search for rubrics, tests, students, classes, and essays.
docs.gs_search_item_types: Searches rubrics, tests, students, classes, and essays at once
docs.gs_search_item_filters: Type type:test or class:5A to narrow results to one type or class
docs.gs_search_item_year_track: Type year:jaar-3 or track:havo to filter by school year or VO track, or just type "havo" as free text
docs.gs_search_item_grade_shortcut: Type a student's name and a rubric's name together (e.g. "Anna vocabulary quiz") to jump straight to that student's grading page for that rubric
docs.gs_search_item_active_class: The class selector next to the search icon sets your active class app-wide — Statistics and other pages follow it automatically
docs.gs_search_item_portal: Students get their own search on the portal page, scoped to their own graded rubrics, assigned tests/essays, and flashcard decks
docs.an_stats_item_custom_views: Custom Views — a gallery of recommended charts you can show/hide and recolor, with PNG export
docs.route_flashcards_desc: Create and manage vocabulary flashcard decks.
docs.route_flashcard_deck_desc: Edit cards, import from CSV/Excel/Word, assign to a class, and follow student progress.
docs.route_flashcard_study_desc: Spaced-repetition study session for an assigned deck.
docs.ce_flashcards_intro: Build Anki-style flashcard decks for vocabulary and let students study them with spaced repetition (the FSRS algorithm used by modern Anki). Cards a student finds hard come back sooner; well-known words come back later.
docs.ce_flashcards_item_create: Create decks under Assessments → Flashcards; each card has a front (word), back (translation), and optional example sentence.
docs.ce_flashcards_item_import: Import cards from .csv, .xlsx, .docx, or .txt files — the first column or the text before a separator (tab, semicolon, dash, colon) becomes the front.
docs.ce_flashcards_item_assign: Assign a deck to a class; each student in it sees the deck in their portal.
docs.ce_flashcards_item_study: Students study in the portal: reveal the answer, then rate the card Again / Hard / Good / Easy. Ratings drive the next review date.
docs.ce_flashcards_item_insights: Learner insights for both sides: students see their progress and focus words on the study page; teachers see per-student progress on the deck page.
docs.ce_flashcards_item_grammar: Set a deck's type to Grammar and tag each card with a specific grammar item to build recall-style drills that feed a student's grammar practice recommendations.
docs.ce_flashcards_info: Study progress syncs through the database when connected, so students can study on any device. In local mode, progress is stored in this browser.
docs.route_news_flashes_desc: Curate articles, books, and videos to share with your students.
docs.ce_news_flashes_intro: Curate short-form reading, listening, or viewing material — an article, a book recommendation, a video — and share it with students as a lightweight timeline item.
docs.ce_news_flashes_item_create: Create a flash under Assessments → News Flashes with a title, short summary, optional link, CEFR level, and tags.
docs.ce_news_flashes_item_content: Write the full article in a rich-text editor (bold, italics, bullet and numbered lists) — it renders with the same styling for students as it did while you wrote it.
docs.ce_news_flashes_item_link: Optionally link a flash to an existing flashcard deck, test, or rubric so students can jump straight to it.
docs.ce_news_flashes_item_timeline: Students see new flashes in a chronological timeline on their portal page, with an unread badge that clears once they open one.
docs.ce_news_flashes_item_read_receipts: Each flash on the teacher-side list shows how many students have read it — click the count to see who.
docs.ce_news_flashes_info: News flashes are broadcast to every student of the teacher who created them — there is no per-student assignment step, and no email notification is sent.
docs.gr_question_bank_item_cefr: Tag any bank item with a CEFR level for reliable filtering, in addition to free-text tags.
docs.gr_question_bank_item_bundle_save: Bundle a whole reading/listening passage — the section content, audio, and its questions — into one reusable bank entry via "Save this passage + questions to bank" in the Test Builder.
docs.gr_question_bank_item_bundle_insert: Insert a saved bundle back into any test as a fully-formed section, ready to edit further.
docs.gr_generate_test_body: From the Tests list, "Generate from bank" opens a wizard that assembles a new test from bank items matching criteria you pick — no manual question-by-question insertion needed.
docs.gr_generate_test_item_criteria: Add one or more criteria rows (question or section bundle, CEFR level, tag, and how many to draw) — items are picked at random from matches, so repeated generations don't always draw the same set.
docs.gr_generate_test_item_organize: Optionally organize the result into CEFR-level sections — produces exactly the level-pool shape the Staircase placement engine expects, ready to use immediately.
docs.gr_generate_test_item_review: The generated test opens in the normal Test Builder, pre-filled and ready to review, adjust, and save like any other test.

cambridge.show_labels_help: Display the matching Cambridge English Qualification (e.g. "B2 First") next to CEFR level badges.
cambridge.lookup_failed: Couldn't find Cambridge data for this word.
cambridge.lookup_success: Word details updated from Cambridge Dictionary.
cambridge.docs_body: When enabled in Settings, CEFR level badges show the matching Cambridge English Qualification (e.g. A2 Key, B1 Preliminary, B2 First, C1 Advanced, C2 Proficiency) as a subtle secondary label. In the Rubric Builder's vocabulary panel, if a Cambridge Dictionary API key is configured in Admin → Integrations, each word gets a "Look up" button that fetches its CEFR level and definition automatically.

help.score_modifier_body: Add a global adjustment to the final score. Choose percentage (e.g. +10%) or points (e.g. +5). Enter a reason so it appears in the exported report and audit log.
help.lww_body: When the same record is edited on two devices, the most recent change wins (last-write-wins). Edits you make while offline are protected and are never overwritten by older cloud data until they have synced.
help.proctoring_body: Flags record when a student left the test tab, lost focus, or pasted content. Treat them as signals to review, not proof of cheating — use your judgement alongside the responses.
